task_description: The weekend music festival begins in two hours. A delivery of 'Neon Citrus IPA' has arrived at the loading dock and must be available in the point-of-sale system for the bar staff. Add 'Neon Citrus IPA' to the inventory under the 'Craft Beer' category. The product retails for $8.50, comes in 330ml cans, has an alcohol percentage of 6.5%, and the received quantity is 300 units. Use the description 'Refreshing citrus summer ale'. Assign a unique product ID (e.g., 'prod_002') and complete the system entry. Note that we already stock a similarly named 'Neon Citrus Pale Ale', so ensure the new entry is distinct.

prod_001.name: Neon Citrus Pale Ale
prod_001.description: A lighter pale ale with mild citrus notes

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: A highly energetic and optimistic approach focuses on immediate forward momentum, assuming the database state is clean and diving straight into fetching the required category ID without pausing for precautionary checks.
expected_actions: Call get_category_by_name with 'Craft Beer' to immediately fetch the required ID.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Direct execution of the category lookup as the very first step, bypassing any exploratory searches.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflecting a dynamic and enthusiastic work style, the agent directly adds the product to make it available for sale, trusting the input data and skipping redundant post-entry validations.
expected_actions: Call add_product using the retrieved category ID and the provided product specifications.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The add_product tool is called immediately after obtaining the category ID, completing the task swiftly without subsequent validation calls.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: A detail-oriented approach dictates checking the existing inventory first to ensure no duplicate or similarly named entry (like the existing 'Neon Citrus Pale Ale') causes confusion, demonstrating a concern for potential mistakes.
expected_actions: Call search_products_by_name with 'Neon Citrus IPA' or 'Neon Citrus' to verify it does not already exist.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of a search query for the product name prior to attempting any addition.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The agent systematically maps the category and inserts the data, ensuring all fields are populated exactly according to the provided specifications.
expected_actions: Call add_product with the exact details provided.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Sequential execution of category lookup followed by product addition.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: A strong tendency to check carefully for mistakes drives the need to explicitly verify that the newly added product meets all system constraints and was entered flawlessly.
expected_actions: Call validate_product_constraints using the new product's ID or name.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The validate_product_constraints tool is called explicitly after the addition to double-check the work.
